VAL-CLK-001.evidence: Boot screenshot shows clock '00:00:16' in HH:MM:SS format in status bar (right-aligned). Hours=00 (valid 00-23), minutes=00 (valid 00-59), seconds=16 (valid 00-59). Clock rendered using proportional font, clearly legible. Code confirms format_time_hms() computes hours/minutes/seconds from elapsed counter ticks.

VAL-CLK-002.evidence: Four screenshots taken across the session show different clock values: 00:00:16 (boot), 00:00:42 (after typing, ~26s later), 00:01:00 (image viewer, ~18s later), 00:01:18 (back to editor, ~18s later). Clock driven by 1-second kernel timer (timer_create with 1_000_000_000 ns). Timer does not block input — typing was responsive throughout.

VAL-CROSS-003.evidence: Clock updated during typing: screenshot at 00:00:42 (after typing 'hello world\ntext editor') and at 00:01:18 (after context switch round-trip + more time). Both screenshots show typed text and different clock times, confirming timer fires independently of input events. Compositor event loop handles both timer and input via sys::wait() on multiple handles.
